articles.title: Domestic cylinder price increased by Rs 29, second rise in three months
articles.description: LPG Price Hike News: The increase in LPG prices comes amid a broader rise in fuel costs. Petrol and diesel prices have been raised by a cumulative Rs 7.50 per litre since mid-May

articles.title: Domestic LPG price raised by Rs 29 per cylinder amid continued pressure on fuel retailers: Report
articles.description: Domestic cooking gas now costs Rs 942 in Delhi after latest hike, following earlier March increase; petrol, diesel and CNG prices also revised amid elevated glo

articles.title: Why is Pentagon worried about Mossad? DNA examines reported US-Israel intelligence tensions
articles.description: The show also focused on India's launch of E-85 fuel, a blend consisting of 85 per cent ethanol and 15 per cent petrol. With sales beginning at select petrol pumps, the analysis explored the fuel's pricing compared to conventional petrol, its potential benefits and drawbacks, and the types of vehicles capable of using the new fuel blend.

articles.title: Maharashtra Govt Alerts Machinery Over Rising Petrol Purchases, Orders Daily Monitoring Of Sales
articles.description: Maharashtra government has directed officials to monitor daily petroleum sales and transportation after noticing increased purchases. The food and civil supplies department invoked 2005 provisions empowering officials to act against illegal storage, black marketing, adulteration and overcharging.

articles.title: ‘US has more oil, gas, coal than any other country’, says Trump
articles.description: Trump stated that the US has the largest energy resources globally, emphasizing success with Venezuela and Iran. He noted rising oil prices but expressed confidence they would drop as oil supply increases, while highlighting US military actions against Iranian threats in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Renault Duster Turbo 1.0 mileage revealed: Is it more efficient than Creta and Seltos?
articles.description: The newly confirmed rating puts the 1.0-litre turbo petrol variant at 19.41 km/l, making it one of the more fuel-efficient petrol-powered SUVs in the midsize category.

articles.title: You can now buy THIS fuel for Rs 82 in Delhi; will it actually save you money?
articles.description: Delhi has launched its first E85 fuel station, with the ethanol-based fuel priced at Rs 82.12 per litre, Rs 20 cheaper than E20 petrol. The government plans to expand E85 availability nationwide, with 500 stations targeted by the end of 2026 and 5,000 by 2027.
